summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two zoning cases. The first was an administrative six-month extension to the final development plan filing deadline for the Eagle's Nest mixed-use development (ZC 2025-006), which was approved unanimously. The second case (ZC 2026-007) involved a major modification to the Lakefield Place PUD to consolidate two PUD sections, convert two residential lots to open space amenity, and address a 2.157-acre outlot along State Route 132; after significant discussion, the commission recommended approval with conditions that differed from the staff recommendation of denial.
decisions: Approved video meeting minutes from June 2, 2026 (Curry yes, Dinwiddie abstain, Lewis yes, Pegram yes)
Recommended approval of ZC 2025-006 Eagle's Nest final development plan six-month filing deadline extension (unanimous yes)
Recommended approval with conditions of ZC 2026-007 Lakefield Place PUD major modification, contrary to staff recommendation of denial (unanimous yes: Curry, Dinwiddie, Lewis, Pegram)
action_items: Applicant (Beaver Creek Site Management/Joe Ferruccio) to submit replat for the 2.157-acre lot split into two lots within the PUD
Applicant to ensure 35% open space benchmark is maintained for the consolidated PUD by trimming lot depths on 15-18 lots
Applicant to record easement for emergency access lane per county and Goshen fire department specifications
Applicant to provide copy of drawings presented at meeting to be included as part of the application record
Lots 201 and 202 to be formally converted to open space/amenity use within the PUD
Drees/Eagle's Nest to continue working on final development plan submissions for commercial portions and apartment complex
key_amounts: 555 residential units - approved in Eagle's Nest preliminary plan
46 acres of open space - Eagle's Nest preliminary plan
2.16 acres (2.157 acres) - outlot along State Route 132
34.9% open space - current consolidated PUD calculation (below 35% benchmark)
35% - required open space benchmark for Lakefield Place PUD
$600,000-$700,000 - estimated value of proposed homes on the outlot lots
notable_topics: Eagle's Nest (former golf course) final development plan six-month filing deadline extension
Lakefield Place PUD major modification to consolidate sections 1-5 and 6-7
Disposition of 2.157-acre outlot along State Route 132 - kept within PUD and split into two residential lots
Conversion of lots 201 and 202 to community amenity and parking open space
Emergency access easement and bollard/gate configuration
Buffer zone requirements along State Route 132
Staff recommended denial of Lakefield Place modification but commission approved with conditions
Discussion of R1 zoning changes and impact on pre-existing applications
attendees: Mr. Frisbee (Zoning Commission member - absent/no response at roll call)
Ms. Purcell (Zoning Commission member - absent/no response at roll call)
Joe Ferruccio (Applicant - Beaver Creek Site Management LLC, 7861 East Kemper Road)
notes: Staff recommended denial of ZC 2026-007 but the commission recommended approval with conditions after extensive deliberation. The commission chose to keep the 2.157-acre outlot within the PUD rather than separating it, avoiding the need for a separate rezoning application. The applicant noted the outlot was always intended to be separate but was inadvertently included in the original PUD approval. The commission discussed at length whether buffering along State Route 132 would be required and concluded it would not be needed since the lot remains within the PUD. Drees is the applicant for Eagle's Nest with Vancooner/Vancouver Homes as owner and McGill Smith Pushun as engineer. Meeting adjourned at 7:16 PM.
title: ZC 7-7-26
